description: Public Python-to-JavaScript SDK parity contract for the @traigent/sdk 0.2.0 parity release.

python.note: This manifest intentionally tracks Python develop, not the currently published Python package. JS 0.2.0 is forward-bound to the next Python public surface unless Python 0.12.0 ships first.

releasePolicy.js020PythonReleaseOrder: JS 0.2.0 may ship before Python 0.12.0. If it does, release notes must describe this as forward-compatible with the next Python public surface, not parity with the currently published Python package.
releasePolicy.publishedPythonCompatibilityNote: Python 0.11.4 at pin time does not expose BenchmarkClient, BenchmarkClientConfig, Dataset, or EvaluationExample. Those symbols are included because the manifest tracks Python develop at f9f1adcb.
refreshCadence: Refresh the manifest on every Python public SDK release candidate or release branch.
Refresh the manifest in the same PR family as any Python change to traigent.__all__.
Refresh the manifest before every JS minor parity release.
If neither SDK releases for a quarter, run a quarterly drift check and open a no-op or update PR documenting the result.

classificationDefinitions.matched: JS exports a production implementation or schema-backed type with the same public intent.
classificationDefinitions.additive-js-alias: JS exports a compatibility alias over an existing camelCase JS type or helper.
classificationDefinitions.deferred-schema: Python exposes the symbol, but JS cannot implement production behavior until TraigentSchema has the contract; JS may expose a throwing migration stub.
classificationDefinitions.deferred-backlog: Tracked for a future JS release or open decision ticket.
classificationDefinitions.out-of-scope-permanent: Python-only runtime, analysis, plotting, validation, or internal surface that is not part of the JS SDK parity contract.

decisions.manifestLocation: TraigentSchema/parity/python-js-sdk.json is authoritative; JS and Python CI read this file instead of maintaining repo-local ignore lists.
decisions.pythonPinning: Only the Python SHA is pinned. JS is validated as any build that satisfies the manifest-required exports.
decisions.js020PythonReleaseOrder: JS 0.2.0 is allowed to ship before Python 0.12.0 as a forward-compatible parity release, provided release notes state that the manifest tracks next Python, not published Python 0.11.4.
decisions.manifestRefresh: TraigentSchema owns manifest refreshes for Python release candidates, Python __all__ changes, JS minor parity releases, and quarterly drift checks when release cadence is slow.
decisions.benchmarkClient: JS 0.2.0 exports a throwing BenchmarkClient stub because Python develop re-added BenchmarkClient, but the benchmark dataset generation endpoint is not yet schema-owned.
decisions.enterpriseAdminClient: JS 0.2.0 keeps the throwing EnterpriseAdminClient migration stub pending Traigent/traigent-js#30.
decisions.observabilityWrappers: Default-client helpers and the observe wrapper are deferred to 0.3.0 to avoid expanding the 0.2.0 parity release with new runtime semantics.
decisions.frameworkDiscovery: Future implicit framework discovery must be opt-in, avoid global patching, log activation, and be deterministic across import order.
